reviews.review_msg: This class is chill, if you have some sort of background in Chinese, it's hard to get a bad grade. The assignments she give is clear, but for the writing quizzes, make sure to study more than just the characters( hanzi) she gives you, she also includes vocabulary from the textbook(kewen) in the quizzes.

A prof who cares about students but.....
Long lectures, not relevant to tests and course material. Solo assignments and quizzes were fine. 
No clear guidelines so its hard to study, exams have questions and vocab we don't study and learn. I was going to take more Chinese but changed my mind after this class..

I think prof Xie is quite knowledgeable in the Chinese language, however, I found the content and her specific teaching less applicable in real-life everyday conversation. I also thought some of her grading was unreasonable. Fair workload, but classes were quite boring and because of this, I decided to no longer keep learning Chinese.
Yo, she s the dopest prof in SFU

Due to small class sizes, Cynthia gives every student individualized feedback/attention which is very helpful. That being said, the workload is very intense and there is a lot more work than expected.

Cynthia is a great prof who really wants her students to do well. As its a small class, she pays attention to the individual growth of every student. A LOT of homework, but that's how you improve in a language. vocab quizzes, unit tests, and in-class participation are required. Carry an open mind and you'll have a good time and see improvement!

Cynthia is a decent prof overall. However, the class was slightly unorganized and left many wondering what is expected for homework and tests. Lectures are mandatory and be prepared to be randomly chosen for participation. This being said, Cynthia explained course concepts clearly was always open for questions.

This is a class for those with Chinese heritage but I am a complete foreigner, so the course was a bit challenging for me. Prof was really helpful and easy to reach out to. This is a language course so it's fair that there are many assignments, be prepared for that.

I took 100200 level Chinese courses with her, and she is amazing. She takes care of her students and encourages them to be better. Because the classes are quite small, we can chat with her a lot like a friend. You just need to memorize a few vocabs and you're good. The listening portion of the tests are a bit hard, but regardless, an easy A.

This professor really cares about her students and is there for her students when they need her. I had an issue with some of my online courses and out of all the professors that I reached out to, she was the only one who truly helped me with my problem. I wish all professors at SFU were like her who genuinely cares about the students.

Had for 180 181 280 281. Teaches with even balance between reading+speaking. Tough language, so you need to put in the time. Completely fair marker; I got a B but was taking 6 courses my first semester while working, would have gotten an A or higher otherwise. Clear, informative and interactive lectures. 2 cheap texts for each level, with workbook

pretty good prof, very nice and understanding, prepares and tells you what will be on a test or exam. has a lot of background information on many words or concepts that she teaches, which helps you understand better. if you keep on top of homework and studying vocab, you'll def get an A in this class. :)

Lots of textbook usage is required, and she gives lots and lots of assignments, as if we wer in HS. She gives a short ltmd time for the quizzes, which I think is way too hard (full page,about 3 paragraphs of chinese reading)& fill in the blanks, even as a chinese heirtage speaker! I hoped that we skip the textbook & teach more aboutchinese cultures

I took the intensive classes CHIN 290+291. She is awesome. The pace/workload/fairness in grading is perfect. Really good at teaching always able to help. Most importantly, she is so clear; it's impossible to misunderstand her. One of my favourite profs.
Excellent teacher, very friendly and dedicated to her students. It is very easy to get along with her and she throws in (and tolerates) plenty of humour in her classes.
